frameworks.name: response gain modulation by attention
frameworks.one_line: attention scales neuronal gain in favor of attended stimuli

frameworks.name: neural representation of stimulus change
frameworks.one_line: attention amplifies coding of abrupt change beyond static feature encoding

frameworks.name: sensory adaptation
frameworks.one_line: adaptation also amplifies change signals but less than attention does

frameworks.name: accuracy-detectability tradeoff in perception
frameworks.one_line: improving change detection comes at the cost of accurate feature representation

core_question: Does attention distort neural representations of sensory changes at the expense of accurate stimulus representation?
key_assumptions: attention modulates neural response gain in visual cortex
neural representations of stimulus features can be read out from population activity
perceptual judgments reflect underlying cortical representations
manipulations: presence vs absence of a motion direction change
comparison of attention effects with adaptation effects
neurophysiology in monkeys plus perceptual reports in humans
key_findings: attention amplifies neural representation of motion direction changes in visual cortex
this amplification exceeds a similar effect produced by adaptation
humans overestimate the magnitude of direction changes perceptually
interpretations: attention distorts representations of abrupt sensory changes, reducing perceptual accuracy
this tradeoff may be an evolutionarily adaptive mechanism prioritizing change detection over fidelity
